hypotheses.hypothesis: Higher-order exchange stabilizes skyrmions even without DMI
hypotheses.result: Lifetimes substantially enhanced; thermal stability achieved in DMI-free systems

hypotheses.hypothesis: Four-spin term modulates both energetic and entropic contributions to decay
hypotheses.result: Energy barrier raises and landscape curvature decreases at saddle point, increasing pre-exponential factor by orders of magnitude

title: Robust photon blockade with hybrid molecular optomechanics
task: Achieve robust photon blockade in hybrid molecular cavity optomechanical systems
key_results: Near-perfect room-temperature photon blockade with arbitrary detuning via degenerate parametric amplification; robust against temperature and optical dissipation; strong photon antibunching demonstrated
comments: Hybrid molecular optomechanics offers compelling advantages, though practical implementation and tuning of the degenerate parametric amplification requires careful engineering.

model_io.model: Hybrid molecular cavity optomechanical system with parametric amplification

hypotheses.hypothesis: Parametric amplification enables robust photon blockade at arbitrary detuning
hypotheses.result: Near-perfect antibunching achieved with arbitrary detuning conditions

hypotheses.hypothesis: Hybrid system maintains photon blockade robustness against thermal noise
hypotheses.result: Room-temperature operation with robust performance against temperature and dissipation

title: Observation of Floquet-induced gap in graphene
task: Observe Floquet-induced band gap in graphene via time-periodic light driving
key_results: Time-resolved ARPES reveals light-induced hybridization gap at Floquet band crossings in monolayer graphene; gap exhibits momentum anisotropy with two protected Dirac nodes; coherent Floquet sidebands observed; gap tunable by light polarization
comments: Long-awaited experimental verification of Floquet engineering in graphene; momentum anisotropy and symmetry-protected nodes add richness to the physics.

model_io.model: Floquet-engineered graphene with resonant light driving

hypotheses.hypothesis: Resonant light driving induces Floquet band hybridization gap in graphene
hypotheses.result: Gap opening observed at band crossings with coherent Floquet sidebands

hypotheses.hypothesis: Spatiotemporal symmetry protects Dirac nodes in gapped Floquet spectrum
hypotheses.result: Two protected Dirac nodes with momentum anisotropy; gap tunable by polarization

title: Magnetic circular dichroism imaging of atomic-scale antiferromagnetic order at a buried interface
task: Map atomic-scale antiferromagnetic order at buried interfaces via electron magnetic circular dichroism
key_results: Atomic-column-resolved eMCD in STEM distinguishes G-type and C-type antiferromagnetic orderings in DyFeO₃ and α-Fe₂O₃; identifies one-unit-cell magnetic dead layer at DyScO₃–SmFeO₃ interface; enhanced signal-to-noise via chirality localization
comments: Novel technique overcomes weak-signal challenge in antiferromagnet imaging; readily accessible implementation enables wide applicability to interfacial magnetism.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Chirality around magnetic atomic columns enhances eMCD signal and noise robustness
hypotheses.result: Atomic-column magnetic characterization achieved with improved SNR

hypotheses.hypothesis: Buried interfaces exhibit magnetic dead layers distinguishable from bulk antiferromagnetism
hypotheses.result: One-unit-cell dead layer identified at DyScO₃–SmFeO₃ interface

title: Universal logical operations in a silicon quantum processor
task: Demonstrate universal logical operations in silicon quantum processor
key_results: [[4,2,2]] code implementation with fault-tolerant logical state preparation, universal gate set, and variational quantum eigensolver execution on H₂O; logical T gate via gate-by-measurement
comments: First realization of logical operations in silicon qubits is a significant milestone, though the 2-qubit demonstration is modest; scalability to larger systems remains challenging.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Phosphorus donor clusters enable fault-tolerant logical qubit operations in silicon
hypotheses.result: Successfully implemented [[4,2,2]] code with full gate set and VQE algorithm

title: Study of half-metallic ferromagnet RhHfVGa for spintronic and thermoelectric applications
task: Investigate half-metallic ferromagnet RhHfVGa for spintronic and thermoelectric applications
key_results: Quaternary Heusler RhHfVGa exhibits 100% spin polarization, 3.00 µB magnetic moment (Slater-Pauling compliant), narrow band gaps (0.98–1.26 eV), high n-type conductivity (3.35–8.43 × 10¹⁹ Ω⁻¹m⁻¹s⁻¹), ZT = 0.82–1.65; Cv = 99.68 J mol⁻¹ K⁻¹ near Dulong-Petit
comments: Comprehensive first-principles study with promising dual functionality; experimental synthesis and property validation needed to confirm computational predictions.

model_io.model: DFT with GGA-PBE96, GGA+U, and mBJ potential

hypotheses.hypothesis: RhHfVGa exhibits half-metallic ferromagnetism with 100% spin polarization
hypotheses.result: Mtot = 3.00 µB matches Slater-Pauling rule; single spin channel for conductivity

hypotheses.hypothesis: Narrow band gaps and high conductivity enable thermoelectric performance
hypotheses.result: ZT ranging 0.82–1.65 across temperatures; strong potential for thermoelectric applications

title: Visual perception of longitudinal waves: theory and observations
task: Study visual perception of longitudinal traveling waves and reveal nonlinearities
key_results: Longitudinal density waves exhibit progressive distortion with amplitude; perceptually split into forward crest and backward trough motion; perceptual cancellation speed scales nonlinearly with oscillation amplitude; motion aftereffect emerges only at reduced forward contrast
comments: Novel psychophysical study revealing emergent perception properties; findings suggest perceptual mechanisms are sensitive to higher-order density fluctuations beyond sinusoidal approximation.

model_io.model: Visual perception model of longitudinal waves

hypotheses.hypothesis: Longitudinal wave motion exhibits nonlinear density distortion at high amplitudes
hypotheses.result: Density function becomes narrow spike at high amplitudes, splitting into double spike at even greater amplitudes

hypotheses.hypothesis: Perception splits into forward crest and backward trough motion rather than uniform travel
hypotheses.result: Perceptual split observed; cancellation speed scales nonlinearly with amplitude

title: Reducing noise levels in eddy current measurements using self-differential probes of the substrate conductivity under a layer of conductive coating in moved objects
task: Reduce noise in eddy current measurements for substrate conductivity under coatings
key_results: Taguchi optimization of self-differential probe parameters reduces noise; rectangular coil design achieves maximum SNR; variance analysis ranks parameter influences; Monte Carlo validation confirms advantages
comments: Practical optimization study with solid experimental validation, though the novelty is primarily in parameter tuning rather than fundamental methodology.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Optimized rectangular coil geometry maximizes SNR for substrate conductivity measurement
hypotheses.result: Rectangular coil design selected as optimal among investigated alternatives

title: Black hole information turbulence and the Hubble tension
task: Explain Hubble tension via fractal information growth in black holes
key_results: Two fractal growth processes yield H₀ = 62.79 ± 4.56 km/s/Mpc (early universe) and 70.07 ± 0.39 km/s/Mpc (late universe), matching observed tension
comments: While the numerical coincidence is striking, the physical mechanism linking black hole entropy to cosmological expansion requires further justification and independent verification.

outputs: Hubble constant estimates (early and late universe)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Hubble tension arises from intrinsic fractal dynamics in early versus late universe
hypotheses.result: Computed H₀ values match observational discrepancy without invoking large-scale structure anomalies

title: In situ spectral reconstruction based on a memristor chip for energy-efficient computational spectrometry
task: Implement energy-efficient computational spectrometry on integrated memristor chip
key_results: 576-Kb memristor chip reconstructs spectra in 125.0 ns consuming 6.7 nJ; 26.5× faster and 162.7× more energy-efficient than state-of-the-art; memristive regularization and filter embedding strategies achieve software-equivalent accuracy despite ill-posed reconstruction
comments: Clever exploitation of memristor physics for analog signal processing; energy/speed gains are substantial, though scalability to larger spectra remains to be demonstrated.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Memristive analog computation with embedded filtering overcomes ill-posedness of spectral inversion
hypotheses.result: Hardware achieves software-equivalent accuracy with 26.5× speed-up and 162.7× energy reduction

title: High-mobility holes in gallium nitride and their quantum oscillations
task: Achieve high hole mobility and quantum oscillations in p-type gallium nitride
key_results: Polarization-induced 2DHG at GaN/AlN interface; light-hole mobility 2,000 cm²V⁻¹s⁻¹ and heavy-hole mobility 400 cm²V⁻¹s⁻¹ at 2 K; Shubnikov–de Haas oscillations extract sheet densities, scattering times, and effective masses for both valence bands
comments: First quantum oscillation observation in p-GaN is significant; enables valence band engineering and cryogenic complementary technology development for quantum control electronics.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Polarization-induced 2DHG in GaN/AlN achieves high hole mobilities
hypotheses.result: Light-hole (2,000 cm²V⁻¹s⁻¹) and heavy-hole (400 cm²V⁻¹s⁻¹) mobilities enable quantum oscillations

hypotheses.hypothesis: Quantum oscillations reveal separate contributions from light and heavy valence bands
hypotheses.result: SdH oscillations extract distinct sheet densities, scattering times, and effective masses

title: LysG-driven transcriptional network rewiring underlies lineage-specific phenotypes in Mycobacterium tuberculosis
task: Identify transcriptional network rewiring driving lineage-specific virulence differences in M. tuberculosis
key_results: LysG induction promotes bedaquiline tolerance in L2 strains; DosR regulon shows 2-3× higher basal expression and stronger nitric oxide response in hyper-virulent L2 versus low-virulence L1
comments: The linking of a single master regulator (LysG) to a clinically relevant drug phenotype is elegant, though causality relies on induction experiments rather than genetic knockout in clinical strains.

outputs: DosR and LysG regulon expression levels

hypotheses.hypothesis: Limited genetic variation in MTBC yields major phenotypic differences through differential transcriptional regulation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: one in three SNPs affected gene expression; transcript-protein correlations were lineage and condition-specific

hypotheses.hypothesis: LysG promotes reduced metabolic activity and bedaquiline tolerance in L2
hypotheses.result: Confirmed by time-course induction experiments under hypoxia and reaeration

title: Engineering ligands for theophylline riboswitches expands its regulatory dynamic range in prokaryotic and eukaryotic systems
task: Optimize theophylline riboswitch ligands to enhance regulatory dynamic range across biological systems
key_results: 4-quinazolinone derivatives achieve 30-fold higher binding affinity and 380-fold ON-state activation in bacteria (vs. 75-fold with theophylline); 70% genome editing efficiency at 10-fold lower concentrations in conditional CRISPR-Cas9 applications
comments: The systematic ligand engineering approach is sound, but the eukaryotic and mycobacterial performance gains (11-fold and 20-fold) are more modest than prokaryotic gains, suggesting context-dependent aptamer accessibility.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Structure-based ligand design increases theophylline aptamer affinity and functional activation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: 30-fold affinity improvement and up to 380-fold expression enhancement in bacteria

hypotheses.hypothesis: Enhanced ligands improve riboswitch performance across diverse organisms
hypotheses.result: Partially confirmed: prokaryotic and conditional CRISPR systems show dramatic gains; eukaryotic activation more modest (11-fold)
